summary_es: Este modulo cubre la simulacion Monte Carlo (MCS) como herramienta fundamental para propagar incertidumbre en modelos financieros. Se revisan los cuatro momentos estadisticos y se demuestra por que la volatilidad es una medida absurda de riesgo que subestima eventos extremos. Se analizan retornos sinteticos tipo S&P 500 para mostrar que no siguen una distribucion normal: tienen colas pesadas (curtosis alta) y asimetria negativa. Se explican la Ley de Grandes Numeros y el Teorema Central del Limite como fundamentos teoricos de MCS. Se aplica MCS a la valoracion de un proyecto de software con multiples factores de riesgo, obteniendo distribuciones completas de NPV, probabilidad de exito y VaR del proyecto.
summary_en: This module covers Monte Carlo simulation (MCS) as the fundamental tool for propagating uncertainty in financial models. The four statistical moments are reviewed and volatility is shown to be a nonsensical risk measure that underestimates extreme events. Synthetic S&P 500-like returns are analyzed to demonstrate non-normality: fat tails (high kurtosis) and negative skewness. Law of Large Numbers and Central Limit Theorem are explained as MCS theoretical foundations. MCS is applied to value a software project with multiple risk factors, obtaining complete NPV distributions, success probability, and project VaR.
learning_objectives_es: Demostrar que los retornos financieros tienen colas pesadas incompatibles con la Normal
Explicar por que la volatilidad subestima el riesgo financiero real
Implementar simulacion Monte Carlo desde estimar Pi hasta valorar proyectos
Aplicar LGN y TLC como fundamentos teoricos de MCS
Construir distribuciones de NPV con multiples factores de riesgo
learning_objectives_en: Demonstrate that financial returns have fat tails incompatible with the Normal distribution
Explain why volatility underestimates real financial risk
Implement Monte Carlo simulation from estimating Pi to project valuation
Apply LLN and CLT as theoretical foundations of MCS
Build NPV distributions with multiple risk factors

parameters.name: Monte Carlo Simulation
parameters.origin_phrase: MCS propagates uncertainty through models to generate outcome distributions

parameters.name: Non-Gaussian Distributions
parameters.origin_phrase: Financial returns exhibit fat tails and negative skewness incompatible with Normal

parameters.origin_phrase: Volatility is a nonsensical risk measure that underestimates financial risk

parameters.name: Trifecta of Errors
parameters.origin_phrase: Assuming normality is a specification error revealed by moment analysis
